heroHeadline: IMF Spring Meetings Open: Georgieva Calls Energy Disruption 'Largest in Modern History'; WEO Apr 14 to Show ~0.8pp Growth Downgrade; FOMC Stagflation Trap Apr 29–30
heroSubtitle: Apr 13: The IMF–World Bank Spring Meetings opened in Washington with 191 countries convening amid the most complex global macro backdrop since 2008. IMF MD Georgieva called the 5-week combined oil/LNG disruption 'the largest disruption to global energy markets in modern history' on CBS Face the Nation (Apr 12). The April 14 WEO will formalize a ~0.8pp growth downgrade from the January 3.3% baseline. FOMC faces a true stagflation trap April 29–30: core PCE 3.06%, headline CPI 3.3%, Michigan sentiment at 47.6 all-time record low. US-China bilateral tariffs remain at 104%/84% — near-embargo levels not seen since 1930s.
footerNote: Data sourced from IMF WEO, BIS, Federal Reserve FOMC, ECB Governing Council, BEA, BLS, World Bank, Tax Foundation, Conference Board, Penn Wharton Budget Model, Yale Budget Lab, University of Michigan Surveys of Consumers, CBOE, Goldman Sachs Research, JPMorgan Global Markets Strategy, Moody's Analytics, Cleveland Fed Inflation Nowcasting
